content.text:
I'll start by exploring the codebase to understand its structure and current state.

<function_calls>
<invoke name="Bash">
<parameter name="command">cd "D:\Proton Drive\My files\Documents\Professional\Programming\Web Development\system.knowledgebase" && ls -la</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>
<thinking>
The user said "Warmup" which seems to be a simple request to get oriented with the codebase. Let me explore the structure.
</thinking>

<function_calls>
<invoke name="Bash">
<parameter name="command">cd "D:\Proton Drive\My files\Documents\Professional\Programming\Web Development\system.knowledgebase" && dir</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>
<thinking>
Let me try with a Windows-compatible command since this is win32.
</thinking>

<function_calls>
<invoke name="Glob">
<parameter name="pattern">D:\Proton Drive\My files\Documents\Professional\Programming\Web Development\system.knowledgebase\*</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>
<thinking>
Let me try a different approach to explore the project structure.
</thinking>

<function_calls>
<invoke name="Glob">
<parameter name="pattern">**/*</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>
<thinking>
Let me try with a simpler pattern first to understand the directory structure.
</thinking>

<function_calls>
<invoke name="Glob">
<parameter name="pattern">*</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>
<thinking>
Now I can see the top-level structure. Let me explore further.
</thinking>

<function_calls>
<invoke name="Read">
<parameter name="file_path">D:\Proton Drive\My files\Documents\Professional\Programming\Web Development\system.knowledgebase\package.json</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>
<thinking>
Good, now I can see the project structure and dependencies. Let me explore the src directory to understand the application architecture.
</thinking>

<function_calls>
<invoke name="Glob">
<parameter name="pattern">src/**/*</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>
<thinking>
Excellent! Now I have a comprehensive view of the project structure. Let me also check the tasks directory to understand what work is in progress.
</thinking>

<function_calls>
<invoke name="Glob">
<parameter name="pattern">tasks/**/*.md</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>
<thinking>
Good, there are active tasks. Let me check the recent git history as per the session protocol.
</thinking>

<function_calls>
<invoke name="Bash">
<parameter name="command">cd "D:/Proton Drive/My files/Documents/Professional/Programming/Web Development/system.knowledgebase" && git log -5 --oneline</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>
<thinking>
Now I have a good understanding of the codebase. Let me summarize what I found.
</thinking>

I've completed the warmup exploration. Here's what I found:

## Codebase Overview: Neuman Brain Dashboard

**Project Type:** React 19 + TypeScript 5.9 + Vite 7 knowledge management dashboard

**Tech Stack:**
- Frontend: React 19, TypeScript 5.9, Vite 7
- State: Zustand 5
- Styling: Tailwind CSS 4
- Storage: IndexedDB (Dexie)
- Testing: Vitest

**Source Structure (`src/`):**
- `components/` - UI components (Dashboard, Layout, Widgets, etc.)
- `features/` - Feature modules (ai-chat, notes, quick-capture, settings, tasks)
- `hooks/` - Custom React hooks (useHotkeys, useMediaQuery, etc.)
- `lib/` - Utilities (security, validation, formatters)
- `stores/` - Zustand state stores
- `types/` - TypeScript type definitions

**Active Tasks:**
- 12 task files in `tasks/` directory
- Recent work: Widget settings, task widgets, UI quick wins

**Recent Commits:**
- Widget settings modal wiring
- Dashboard task widget improvements
- UI quick wins sprint
- Massive task cleanup (76% reduction)
- Widget component refactoring milestone

**Key Patterns:**
- Widget-based dashboard architecture
- Local-first, privacy-focused design
- Zustand for global state management
- Feature-based folder organization

Ready to assist with any specific requirements or exploration needs.
